title: RigidGroup Object
description: Represents a rigid group within an assembly.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createForAssemblyContext
methods.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

methods.name: setOccurrences
methods.description: Sets which occurrences are to be part of this rigid group.   To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this group. This can be accomplished using the following code: group.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

properties.name: assemblyContext
properties.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this rigid group.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the RigidGroup object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same rigid group.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: isSuppressed
properties.description: Gets and sets if this rigid group is suppressed.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets whether the occurrences that are part of this rigid group are visible or not.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of the rigid group as seen in the timeline.

properties.name: nativeObject
properties.description: The NativeObject is the object outside the context of an assembly and in the context of it's parent component. Returns null in the case where this object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: occurrences
properties.description: Returns the list of occurrences that are part of the rigid group.

properties.name: parentComponent
properties.description: Returns the parent component that owns this rigid group.

properties.name: timelineObject
properties.description: Returns the timeline object associated with this rigid group.

related_samples.name: Rigid Group API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Rigid Group.

Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = rigidGroup_var.assemblyContext

When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. classType.title: RigidGroup.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createForAssemblyContext.title: RigidGroup.createForAssemblyContext Method
createForAssemblyContext.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.
createForAssemblyContext.return_description: Returns the proxy object or null if this isn't the NativeObject.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that defines the context to create the proxy in.

deleteMe.title: RigidGroup.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.return_description: Returns True if the delete was successful.

setOccurrences.title: RigidGroup.setOccurrences Method
setOccurrences.description: Sets which occurrences are to be part of this rigid group. To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this group. This can be accomplished using the following code: group.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

parameters.name: occurrences
parameters.description: An ObjectCollection containing the occurrences to use in creating the rigid group.

parameters.name: includeChildren
parameters.description: Boolean indicating if the children of the input occurrences should be included in the rigid group.
